advisory.title: ASA-2026-002: Cosmos EVM ICS20 precompile allows repeated use of token balances
advisory.description: Cosmos EVM ICS20 precompile contains a state-handling flaw across nested EVM execution paths. State updates from recursive precompile calls were not reflected in the outer execution context, allowing a transaction to spend the same token balance multiple times via nested ICS20 precompile calls. The bug was exploited on the Saga network for roughly $7M before mitigation. Fixed in v0.6.0 by reworking state consistency in precompiles/ics20/ics20.go and precompiles/ics20/tx.go.

introducingCommits.subject: imp(all): Add EVM, ERC-20, feemarket and precompiles from Evmos (#37)

timeline.notes: vulnerableHead is the v0.5.1 release commit, the last vulnerable cosmos/evm release before the v0.6.0 patch. The introducing commit is the commit that imported the precompiles from Evmos, which is when precompiles/ics20/ics20.go (containing the affected nested-execution state handling) entered the cosmos/evm tree.

expectedOutcome.description: Scanner should flag incorrect state handling in the ICS20 precompile (precompiles/ics20/ics20.go and tx.go) where nested EVM execution paths fail to commit state updates to the outer context, allowing the same token balance to be spent multiple times in a single transaction.

checks.name: advisory_published
checks.details: ASA-2026-002 (GHSA-54gx-3cgr-7mfm) was published on 2026-03-09 with the patch in cosmos/evm v0.6.0.

checks.name: vulnerable_head_in_advisory_range
checks.details: vulnerableHead is the v0.5.1 release commit, the latest tag in the <0.6.0 vulnerable range.
